standard_name: Abu Dhabi Healthcare Information and Cyber Security Standard (ADHICS)

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, enforce and maintain a human resources security policy covering the security aspects of employment and termination 1. Define management requirements on; a. Background verification for employees and contractors b. Roles and responsibilities c. Compliance with acceptable usage and other organizational security policies d. Training and awareness needs e. Return of assets during exit

criteria.text: Mandate the requirements of non-disclosure and confidentiality during and after employment 3. Include reference to organizational disciplinary process Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall conduct background verification checks on all candidates for employment, contractors and third-party users 1. Define background verification process addressing provisions of government mandates and entity demands

criteria.text: Establish criteria for background verification checks based on:
sub: Role of the individual
Classification of information access needed c. Access to critical areas
Risk identified Basic

subcontrols: Role of the individual
Classification of information access needed c. Access to critical areas
Risk identified Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish  specific terms and condition of employment The terms and condition shall: 1. Include control requirement specific to employees, contractors and third parties, relevant to their roles and risk profiles

criteria.text: Include information security responsibilities of the healthcare entity and of the employees, contractors and third parties 3. Include standard information security requirements
Be read, understood, agreed and signed by employees, contractors and third parties
Conduct mandatory briefing sessions to employees, contractors and third parties on standard and specific information security requirements of the terms and condition
Maintain adequate records on employee, contractor and third party briefing

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity management shall ensure employees, contractors and third party users adopt and apply security in accordance with established entity policies and procedures 1. Ensure employees, contractors and third party users are briefed on the entity’s information security compliance requirements

criteria.text: Establish acceptable usage policy and ensure users read, accept and sign the policy prior to the provision of system, application or information access 3. Consider segregation of duties to avoid potential misuse of position or conflict of interest Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop new or modify existing awareness and training  programs to include requirements of governmental and organizational information security demands 1. Ensure all employees and where relevant contractors and third parties receive appropriate awareness and training to enhance the entity’s security posture and to minimize probabilities of information security risks

criteria.text: Ensure that an awareness and training program is formally launched and professionally managed 3. Enhance training contents and enrich delivery of awareness aspects based on evolving needs
Evaluate effectiveness and maintain appropriate record of awareness and trainings delivered Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify and address skill and competency demands and gaps 1. Assess and identify skill and competency gaps on information security demands

criteria.text: Implement skill and competency development programs Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall conduct periodic security awareness campaigns, based on established yearly schedules 1. Conduct awareness campaign for general and targeted user groups

criteria.text: Identify innovative methods and medium to communicate security requirements 3. Include incentive programs for user participation and adherence to security practices The awareness campaign shall:
Present current risks around the work and industry, and ways to address
Present learning from incident
Demonstrates the need to protect healthcare information

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce a disciplinary process for employees, where relevant contractors and third parties, who have committed security breaches 1. Ensure employees, contractors and third party resources are aware of the entity’s disciplinary processes

criteria.text: Enforce disciplinary processes and maintain necessary records on the breaches and on management’s actions Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define responsibilities concerning information security for performing employment termination or change of employment 1. Establish internal and external communication protocol on employment exit

criteria.text: Ensure adequate knowledge transfers and responsibility handovers Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure recovery of all organizational assets upon termination of employment, contract or agreement 1. Ensure all organizational assets are recovered and necessary acknowledgement and clearance obtained from appropriate stakeholders

criteria.text: Ensure all information, with special focus on healthcare information, has been recovered and cannot be misused anywhere, anytime 3. Ensure resources leaving the entity formally acknowledges and conforms that no information is under their direct or indirect possession or use Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall remove access rights and revoke privileges of individuals upon termination of employment, contract or agreement The healthcare entity shall remove access to systems, applications, information, secure areas, and work areas. 1. Ensure access to systems, application, information, secure areas, work areas and identified critical areas are revoked upon termination

criteria.text: Communicate with health sector regulator or Abu Dhabi government to revoke any relevant system and application access upon termination Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop internal process to manage internal transfers and change of role 1. Ensure communication to all necessary internal and external stake holders on change of role or internal transfers

criteria.text: Revoke access and privileges associated with old role and reassign privileges on system, application and information access and utilization consistent with their new role based on necessary authorization Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, implement and maintain an asset management policy to: 1. Be relevant and appropriate for entities operational and risk environment

criteria.text: Establish framework to effectively manage the entity’s information assets through ownership assignment, accountability & responsibility definition, recording and maintaining of all/relevant properties of asset 3. Define roles and responsibilities for actions expected out of asset management policy, and shall have functional KPI’s for business/function leaders
Define and enforce classification schemes, as applicable for AD Health Sector (Public, Restricted, Confidential & Secret)
Identify requirements of data retention, handling and disposal
Have provisions to manage Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) arrangements

controls.control_demand: Where applicable, the healthcare entity shall pay specific attention to medical equipment and devices while defining policy, and shall categorically address the following demands: 1. Roles that will be allowed to access, use and maintain medical devices and equipment shall be established

criteria.text: To the extent possible, medical devices and equipment to authenticate users, based on healthcare entity authentication and authorization process 3. The need for handling procedures for each medical device and equipment in use shall be defined and updated as required to stay current
The need to establish and maintain risk log concerning medical devices and equipment
Decommissioning and/or disposal of medical devices and equipment Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall have all their information assets identified, recorded and maintained through an information asset inventory. 1. The inventory shall be updated periodically, or during change in the environment, and shall be accurate and reliable

criteria.text: The inventory can be centralized or distributed (function/line-of- business/service wise) based on the entity’s internal structures, and shall be updated 3. The inventory shall establish the relations between various types of information assets, in support of care delivery; Sample illustration: Service A  needs B Information  supplied by C Device/Equipment/Process/Dependent-Service  processed using D Application (ERP/EMR/Office Automation Applications/etc.)  running on E Technology (server/systems)  supported/operated/managed by XYZ Roles (human resources involved in care delivery) Basic

controls.control_demand: Ownership for each identified assets shall be assigned to a designated role: 1. The owner of an information asset shall define/identify the control requirements to minimize the impact of risk, due to the compromise of assets under his ownership

criteria.text: The owner shall review the adequacy of implemented control measures periodically and amend/modify the control environment as necessary 3. The owner shall ensure effectiveness of the implemented controls, in addressing the risk environment
Access and/or use of information assets shall be authorized by the asset owner

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce rules on the acceptable use of information assets: 1. The rules shall be communicated to all employees and contractors in support of care delivery, and shall be read and acknowledged by all

criteria.text: Entities shall maintain records of user acceptance on the acceptable use of information assets The rule shall consider general requirements and industry best practices and shall have management requirements to reduce probabilities of information leakage/loss/theft and system compromises. Basic

controls.control_demand: Entity management shall be aware of emerging cyber risks, and shall address risk due to the exploitation of the concept-in-practice “Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)” 1. Probabilities of compromise through the use of personal devices shall be addressed through suitable rules and role-based usage agreements

criteria.text: Authorization to use personal devices to access/view/use/share/process/store personal health information is subject to user acknowledgement on the usage agreements Control process and technology solution shall be implemented to reduce/address/contain factors of risk. Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall classify  all information assets, that categorises information assets into one of the following classification scheme:  Public  Restricted  Confidential  Secret Basic

Information classification shall consider value of the information and shall be more restrictive/deterrent based on the entity’s tolerance of financial impact due to compromise of the information considered. Transitional
The level of essential protection needed for an asset shall be considered while determining asset classification. Transitional
The healthcare entity shall establish process to reassess and/or change information classification, based on the following: 1. Change in the value of information

criteria.text: Changes to environment (location, access, storage, processing, usage, etc.) 3. Changes in protection levels Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish process to interpret classification schemes, while receiving information from other entities/3rd parties and shall apply all essential control measures to safeguard/protect against compromise. Transitional

The healthcare entity shall establish criteria for automated classification of information and shall consider using technology solutions to do so based on established classification scheme and criteria. Advanced
The healthcare entity shall establish process to label its information assets in all its form (physical & digital) in a way that is consistent with its classification scheme. Basic

Handling procedures shall be defined for information, consistent with their classification. 1. Handling procedures shall detail security requirements during:  Access granting and privilege allocation  Processing  Storing  Communication/sharing  Printing

criteria.text: Security requirements based on asset value shall be considered in the handling procedures Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall manage removable media in accordance with the classification scheme, handling procedures and acceptable use of assets. The entity shall: 1. Establish media management procedures to address lifecycle requirements (setup, distribution, utilization and disposal)

criteria.text: Implement rules and guidelines for protecting assets against unauthorised access, misuse or corruption during movement. Basic

controls.control_demand: Access and usage of removable media shall be controlled and shall be based on the entity’s management approval. Entity management shall: 1. Accept all involved/inherent risk concerning the use of removable media, and shall bear all responsibilities and is held accountable for the risks inherent in authorizing the use of removable media Basic
The healthcare entity shall establish medical devices and equipment management procedures for each category of identified medical devices and equipment. Basic

Access and privilege allocation for medical devices shall be provided to defined roles, with essential qualification and experience required to operate. Secure and safe-guard medical devices and equipment in accordance with its classification scheme and risk factor Basic

The healthcare entity shall prevent unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction or loss of patient health information stored on medical devices and equipment. Entities shall ensure; 1. Information stored within the medical devices and equipment shall be encrypted

criteria.text: Electronic communication between medical devices and equipment shall be encrypted 3. Healthcare entities shall define minimum essential qualification required to operate and/or handle medical devices and equipment
Copies of valuable health data is moved to a secure storage/location to reduce the risk of its data damage or loss Transitional

controls.control_demand: Healthcare facilities shall consider wired communication facility for medical devices and equipment. Usage of wireless communication facility with medical devices and equipment shall be avoided to the extent possible. Transitional
Entity shall deploy technology solution to white list removable media, and shall be complemented by content encryption and biometric based access provisioning. Advanced

The healthcare entity shall establish control procedures for the removal, movement, and transfer of information assets (information, equipment, medical devices, and information processing equipment/systems). Healthcare entities shall; 1. Authorize removal, movement and transfer of information assets

criteria.text: Maintain records of removal, movement and transfer Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall dispose information assets, when no longer required:  by the entity  on basis of regulatory demands  for legal proceedings Basic
The healthcare entity shall establish a control process that ensures data once destroyed is not recovered Basic
Media, both digital and physical, when no longer required shall be destroyed by the entity Basic

The healthcare entity shall establish control procedures for the secure disposal or reuse of media, equipment, devices and systems, containing classified information. 1. Ensure sensitive data and licensed software has been securely removed beyond recovery, prior to disposal Transitional
All disposal requirement shall be authorized by entity management prior to disposal Basic

The healthcare entity shall maintain records, on media disposal. The records shall have, but not be limited to, the following fields:  Information and/or asset owner  Type of media  Classification  Disposal type  Reason for disposal  Retention expiry date (if data)  Data removal confirmation and evidence  Disposal authorized by Advanced

The healthcare entity shall develop, implement and maintain a physical and environmental security policy, to ensure adequate physical and environmental protection of entities information assets. 1. Be relevant and appropriate for entities operational and risk environment, concerning internal and external threats

criteria.text: Address requirements of secure storage of hazardous or combustible materials that ensures avoidance of:  human injuries or loss of life  damage to information and information systems 3. Consider classification of information assets and their physical presence
Define roles and responsibilities for actions expected out of physical and environmental security policy
Be reviewed, updated and maintained at planned intervals or during significant changes to operating or risk environment, whichever is earlier
Be read and formally acknowledged by all users

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish procedures and guidelines in support of policy implementation Transitional

The physical and environmental policy shall consider equipment and medical devices, with specific focus on their: 1. Physical and environmental demands, as needed by the manufacturer recommendations and regulatory requirements

criteria.text: Placement and physical access 3. Probabilities of data loss during maintenance, decommissioning and/ or authorized off-site activities Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define and use security perimeters to protect facilities that contain information and information systems. 1. Identify secure areas, and define security perimeter, based on information assets contained within or information being processed

criteria.text: Ensure adequate security counter measures are applied to identified secure areas to protect information and information systems within 3. Secure areas of medical equipment and devices hosting or usage to avoid and minimize probabilities of unauthorized access and usage
Consider the impact of compromise of confidentiality, integrity and availability of information or information assets while applying security counter measures Basic

controls.control_demand: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate control measures to ensure only authorized personnel are provided access and authorized activities are being conducted. 1. Maintain List of authorised personnel having access to secure areas

criteria.text: Authenticate all persons accessing secure areas 3. Maintain records for secure area access
Ensure that all employees and contractors wear distinguished form of visible identification (Badge/ID cards) within the premises of the entity
Ensure the locking mechanisms on all access doors are adequate, and alarms configured to alert prolonged open-state of doors
Escort contractors or third parties while inside the secure areas

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall nominate owners for each identified secure areas. Nominated owners of secure area shall: 1. Review access records/logs and surveillance footage at least on a quarterly basis

criteria.text: Reconcile list of authorized users, having access to secure areas 3. Maintain a list of physical key inventory, as with whom the keys of secure areas are with Transitional
Avoid obvious signs that indicates the type of information or activities in the secure areas Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall design and apply physical protection against natural disasters, environmental threats, external attacks and/or accidents. 1. Ensure that fall-back equipment, device, system and backup media are protected from damage caused by natural or man-made disasters

criteria.text: Battery power backup shall be available to provide power to key information systems and critical data centre infrastructures Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure that physical and environmental protection countermeasures and procedures applied are aligned with the outcome of Risk Assessment and regulatory mandates. Transitional
The healthcare entity shall design physical protection and guidelines for working in secure areas. 1. Supervise activities in secure areas

criteria.text: Control access of mobile, portable and surveillance devices/equipment/utilities, to secure areas Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall have segregated delivery and loading areas and shall establish control measures over entry and exit. 1. Establish access procedures to loading and unloading areas to restrict access to only authorized personnel

criteria.text: Inspect and register incoming and outgoing materials, in accordance with healthcare entity’s asset management procedures Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall site/position equipment and medical devices in manner that they are always protected. 1. Establish guidelines on physical protection and unauthorized access of equipment and medical devices

criteria.text: Consider environmental risk condition while positioning of equipment and medical devices Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall protect equipment and medical devices from disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities. The healthcare entity shall; 1. Ensure uninterrupted power provisions to information processing systems Transitional

The healthcare entity shall maintain supporting equipment, to ensure their continued availability. 1. Document suppliers’ recommendations for the maintenance of equipment and make them available to maintenance personnel.

criteria.text: Establish operating procedures for commissioning, maintenance and decommissioning of equipment activities 3. Establish maintenance schedule of supporting utilities, and maintain up- to date records for maintenance carried out Advanced

controls.control_demand: Power, telecommunication and cables carrying data shall be secured and protected. 1. Ensure that power, telecommunication and data cables are protected against physical tampering

criteria.text: Segregate power and telecommunication/data cables  to avoid interference Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify and apply security measures to protect equipment, medical devices and information processing systems while off- site. 1. Ensure manufacturer’s recommendation and instructions are followed, while equipment, medical devices and information processing systems are off-site

criteria.text: Ensure that movement and possession (chain of custody) logs for off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems maintained and verified 3. Ensure security measures are applied to protect off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems from probabilities of information leakage, tampering and unauthorized activities Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure that unattended equipment, medical devices and information processing systems are protected against information leakage and unauthorized activities. 1. Define user responsibilities and establish procedures when leaving equipment, medical devices and information processing systems unattended

criteria.text: Implement controls to protect equipment, medical devices and information processing systems when left unattended Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define and enforce a clear desk and clear screen policy to paper documents, removable storage media, and information processing systems. The clear desk and clear screen policy shall: 1. Define user responsibilities with respect to clear desk and clear screen requirements

criteria.text: Be appropriate to the purpose and objectives of the healthcare entity 3. Read and acknowledged by all employees and contractors of the healthcare entity Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall implement a formal user registration and de- registration process. 1. Ensure request for user registration and de-registration are process driven, and are in compliance with established criteria for access

criteria.text: Ensure unique user accounts are created for each individual requiring access, and prohibit sharing of same account with multiple users 3. Ensure group user account are not created or used
Revoke user accounts during employee exit
Revalidate access requirements during role change
Maintain records/list of persons authorised to use healthcare entity’s information systems, applications, medical devices and equipment

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall restrict and control allocation of privileges, based on principles of need to know. 1. Ensure normal user accounts are not used as service accounts or used to conduct privileged application and system level activities

criteria.text: Privilege or administrative accounts shall be used by individual with a role to conduct privilege activities 3. Ensure users privileges are restrictive in nature, and are assigned based on needs to conduct business activities
Privilege or administrative accounts shall not be used for conducting normal day to day operational activity
Ensure usage of service accounts are controlled, and are not hardcoded in application codes are scripts
Enforce multifactor authentication scheme for all administrative  access

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish process for secure allocation, use and management of security credentials. 1. Ensure default application and system passwords are changed and not being used

criteria.text: Ensure passwords are always hashed and stored in encrypted format 3. During initial user account creation, communicate details of user account and password in two different communication modalities
Enforce complexity requirements on password characters, and shall have at least:
sub: Eight characters
One number, one upper-case and lower-case character, and a special character

criteria.text: Enforce passwords, including that of service accounts and privileged accounts, are recycled at an entity-defined time frame
Ensure that password history is maintained, and shall restrict users from using immediately used previous passwords (at least 3 previous passwords)

subcontrols: Eight characters
One number, one upper-case and lower-case character, and a special character

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall protect confidential and secret information on portable or removable media, mobile or portable devices, and medical equipment or devices. 1. Authenticate user, where relevant, access to equipment, devices and media

criteria.text: Ensure media containing confidential and secret information is password protected and encrypted 3. Where relevant, control access to medical equipment and devices through password enforcement in compliance with the healthcare entities password complexity and usage requirements
Control access to mobile and portable devices hosting confidential and secret information
Establish mobile device management process to protect entity information being used, processed or stored in mobile devices Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall control access to equipment, devices, system and facilities at teleworking sites. 1. Ensure access to equipment, devices, system and facilities at teleworking sites are authenticated, and their access to entity resources are authorized based on need

criteria.text: Ensure confidentiality and protection of healthcare and personal health information while providing/consuming services through teleworking principles, including telemedicine related services 3. Conduct random audit of equipment, devices, system and facilities at teleworking sites
Maintain an inventory of assets in use at teleworking sites Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall review access and privileges granted to its user. 1. Establish process for the reviewing of user access and associated privileges to various entity resources

criteria.text: Define responsibility for access and privileges review, based on entity resources being accessed 3. Conduct user access and privileges review at least once a year or earlier, as required by the entity’s risk environment
Maintain an up to date inventory of access granted and privileges assigned
Define the criteria for the automatic revocation of user access and privileges based on the entity’s defined period of inactivity or non-usage of resources Basic

controls.control_demand: Access to the entity’s network and network services shall be controlled, and shall be provided based on specific need for which the user is authorized for Basic
The healthcare entity shall use appropriate authentication methods to control access of remote users 1. Ensure all remote login and access are only through secure channels

criteria.text: Consider usage of multifactor authentication scheme to control access by remote users Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify all equipment and devices connected to its network, and shall have automated mechanism to detect unauthorized equipment and devices Basic

The healthcare entity shall control access for the purpose of diagnostic and configuration 1. Identify and whitelist all ports, services and utilities that are used for troubleshooting, and for diagnostics and configuration purposes

criteria.text: Define protection mechanism for the diagnostic and configuration services and utilities that are essential, and disable services and utilities that are not required. 3. Restrict access for remote troubleshooting, diagnostic and configuration to authorized roles and shall be allowed from authorized workstations
Log all remote access activities related to troubleshooting, diagnostic and configuration Advanced

controls.control_demand: User access to shared and isolated networks shall be restricted 1. Provide access to shared and isolated networks in line with its Access Control Policy, requirements of business applications and need to access shared resources Basic

The healthcare entity shall define and implement network routing controls to ensure information flow and system, devices, equipment connections are not compromised and are in line with requirements of Access Control Policy 1. Establish processes for secure configuration and rules application for network routing requirements

criteria.text: Always ensure source and destination address and services or ports are used while defining and applying routing rules 3. Enable routing protection countermeasures to avoid manipulation of routing systems and tables
Define and implement network architecture that segregates and isolates internal and publically accessible systems.
External connections to information systems and networks outside entity shall be managed through interfaces consisting of perimeter protection devices (such as firewalls)
Ensure that communications with external systems, networks and key internal systems are always monitored for malicious and suspicious payloads

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure wireless access within the entity is secured. 1. Establish usage restrictions and secure configuration requirements

criteria.text: Establish authorization process for wireless access and usage 3. Ensure public and guest access are segregated and isolated from the entity’s internal network
Ensure that internal wireless is not broadcasted
Authenticate wireless connections using strong encryption mechanism and  based on entity’s internal authentication scheme
Control privileged and administrative activities are not carried out through the entity’s  wireless network

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce secure log-on and log-off procedures to control access to system and applications. 1. Ensure that access to entities systems, applications and services that process, use or store healthcare information are authenticated

criteria.text: Enforce automated locking of workstation/system after a predefined period of inactivity 3. Establish authorization procedures, based on classification of systems, application, services and information in scope
Establish and enforce idle session time-out requirements
Automatically terminate inactive sessions after a predefined period of session inactivity
Display a logon banner that requires the user to acknowledge and accept security terms and their responsibilities before access to the system is granted Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall create unique identifier (user ID) for each users who require access to entities systems, applications or services, and shall implement a suitable authentication technique. 1. Grant each user with a unique identifier

criteria.text: Ensure all user activities are logged with the associated identifier Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall restrict and control the use of utility programs and tools that might be capable of overriding system and application controls. 1. Identify essential system utilities and tools and enforce appropriate controls for use

criteria.text: Provide access to system utilities and tools based on appropriate authorization 3. Maintain inventory of access to system utilities and tools
Monitor use of system utilities and tools Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall restrict access to information and application system functions in accordance with the access control policy. 1. Ensure access to information and application access is restricted and based on need-to-know principles and appropriate authorization

criteria.text: Enforce role-based access mechanisms 3. The need for access shall be justified by individuals responsibilities
Ensure that office assistants, cleaning staffs and other unauthorized personnel do not have access to healthcare data Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall isolate sensitive systems in a dedicated environment. Transitional

The healthcare entity shall implement controls and shall not expose non-public information to the general public. 1. Establish and enforce procedures for publishing of public information to ensure non-public information is not exposed accidently or deliberately

criteria.text: Establish and enforce procedure to periodically validate that non-public information is not exposed to the public domain 3. Validate relevance of publicly available information
Ensure no healthcare and related data is exposed to the public domain Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, enforce and maintain an operations management policy to ensure support and maintenance activities concerning data, technology and application are controlled. 1. Be relevant and appropriate to the healthcare entity’s operational and risk environment concerning data, technology and application

criteria.text: Establish management demands on: 3.
sub: Segregation of duties
Configuration management c. Change control
Baselines and minimum security configurations
Standard operating procedures f. Capacity management
System acceptance
Malware control i. Quality management j. Backup management
Logging and monitoring l. Patch management Provide framework for managing operational activities Basic

subcontrols: Segregation of duties
Configuration management c. Change control
Baselines and minimum security configurations
Standard operating procedures f. Capacity management
System acceptance
Malware control i. Quality management j. Backup management
Logging and monitoring l. Patch management Provide framework for managing operational activities Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop and enforce baseline and recommended configuration settings for common information technology products and applications, medical devices and equipment The healthcare entity, while developing baseline and recommended configuration setting, shall consider: 1. Manufacturer’s security recommendations

criteria.text: Requirements of this Standard 3. Industry best practices
Risk mitigation strategies
Corrective and preventive actions (audit, assessment and incident outcomes) Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall document operating procedures for all support, operational and maintenance activities of information systems and application, medical devices and equipment 1. Disseminate operating procedures and ensure all relevant stakeholders are aware of their responsibilities as needed by their role

criteria.text: Ensure operating procedures are relevant and are updated on defined timelines or when essential
Ensure system documentation includes up-to-date diagrams. Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall control changes to information systems and application, medical devices and equipment 1. Establish a Change Advisory Board to authorize changes

criteria.text: Define and enforce a change management process that addresses the following elements: 3.
sub: Identification and recording of significant changes
Planning and testing of changes c. Assessment of potential impacts
Formal approval procedure
Communication of change to all relevant stakeholders f. Roll-back plan to be utilized during unsuccessful changes
Post implementation assessment

Maintenance of previous version of software, code and configurations Define information systems and applications, medical devices and equipment that shall be covered by the Change Management Process Transitional

subcontrols: Identification and recording of significant changes
Planning and testing of changes c. Assessment of potential impacts
Formal approval procedure
Communication of change to all relevant stakeholders f. Roll-back plan to be utilized during unsuccessful changes
Post implementation assessment

Maintenance of previous version of software, code and configurations Define information systems and applications, medical devices and equipment that shall be covered by the Change Management Process Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish a process that controls transition of information systems and applications 1. Ensure that movement of system and applications from development or project state to operational or production state are managed through the Authorization and Change Process Transitional
The healthcare entity shall identify and segregate roles of conflicting interests and assign responsibilities accordingly

criteria.text: Implement suitable alternative or compensating controls when roles of conflicting interests cannot be assigned to different individuals Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify and separate development, test, staging and operational environments 1. Identify the appropriate level of protection between operational, staging, test, and development environments

criteria.text: Document and apply clear processes for the transfer of data, information, code, configuration, software and systems between environments 3. Ensure as-is operational data is not used in test environment
Restrict usage/migration of test data into operational environment Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify and document current and future capacity requirements while planning for new information systems and applications 1. Have the ability to monitor and measure the capacity of current systems and estimate future information systems and application demands

criteria.text: Ensure there is sufficient capacity with information systems to support good system performance and reliability. 3. Identify capacity thresholds for all information systems and applications, and shall define advance escalation matrix to ensure capacity demands are met
Establish process to:
sub: decommission systems that are no longer needed
optimise databases c. archive data that is not accessed regularly Advanced

subcontrols: decommission systems that are no longer needed
optimise databases c. archive data that is not accessed regularly Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish acceptance criteria for new information systems and applications, changes, upgrades and releases, in addition to satisfactory test results 1. Establish processes for system acceptance, and ensure system acceptance is acknowledged by the relevant authoritative individual

criteria.text: Develop test cases for each of the requirements and changes and ensure tests are carried out and test results documented prior to usage in an operational environment 3. Ensure testing is never performed on production systems
Ensure user profiles (with permissions appropriate for the tasks) used for testing are different from the ones used for operational and development activities
Ensure development tools and/or editors are not installed on operational systems Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall protect its information assets from malware 1. Ensure minimum security configurations is maintained in all information assets, as applicable and as relevant

criteria.text: Implement anti-malware and anti-virus protection mechanisms for network and individual information systems (server, workstation, mobile/portable computing devices) 3. Ensure anti-malware and anti-virus protections mechanisms are updated and current
Enable real-time protection capabilities
Establish and enforce periodic scan schedules
Scan removable media for viruses and malware on all occasions when they are connected to information systems

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall deploy gateway level protection mechanisms to detect and defend against malware and viruses 1. Deploy gateway level protection for web and email traffic from and to the entity

criteria.text: Implement technology that can detect and prevent access to malicious websites or sites from prohibited categories. Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall maintain backup copies of essential information and software needed to support care delivery and its operations 1. Establish backup management process that identifies; a. Essential and critical information in support of care delivery, business and entity operations b. Data owner c. Data recovery point and time requirements d. Backup frequencies, time of execution and methods e. Data restoration frequencies and test criteria

criteria.text: Ensure backup of all identified essential and critical data 3. Ensure data restoration requirements for continuity and recovery are adequately met Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish data archival requirements that satisfies entities retention demands 1. Establish formal processes for archival and destruction of data

criteria.text: Identify data-sets and establish retention requirements as needed by law, regulation, and entity demands 3. Identify and enforce archival criteria (what and when to archive, how long to archive) and methods (physical/electronic) that satisfies established retention timelines
Preserve data during archival
Destroy data that has crossed retention timelines and are no longer required by the entity
Maintain adequate record on archival and destruction Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce monitoring procedures for information systems and application, medical devices and equipment Monitoring procedures shall: 1. Identify aspects (system use, changes, unauthorized activities, internal processing, exception, information exchange, integration, access, etc.) to be monitored

criteria.text: Establish frequency and methods (dashboards, web-link, mobile-app, scheduled tasks, parameter validation, logs, records, manual verification, etc.) of monitoring 3. Establish minimum information gathering requirements for each monitoring activities
Define minimum time requirements for maintaining information gathered from monitoring activities
Define criteria for alerting and escalation
Have defined criteria that quantifies specific outcomes of monitoring as incidents

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall enable audit logs recording administrator, operator and user activities, exceptions and security events. The log shall include faults related to information processing and communication 1. Identify all activities to be captured in logs for all hardware devices, equipment, operating systems and applications

criteria.text: Identify minimum required information to be logged 3. Define minimum frequency requirements for reviewing each type of log
Define minimum time requirements for maintaining each type of log commensurate with legal, regulatory and entity demands
Ensure that logs are not tampered with or modified or destroyed
Ensure unauthorized access to logs are controlled Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall preserve logs in a centralized log management system 1. Control access to the centralized log management solution

criteria.text: Ensure the centralized log management solution is managed by individuals who do not have operational role in implementing or maintain information systems or application 3. Retain logs for a period commensurate with legal, regulatory and entity demands on each type of log
Define use cases and dashboards based on the entity’s needs and industry recommendations, and shall consider:
sub: System utilisation and performance trends
Deviation from entity policy and procedures c. Access control variances and violations
Any potential sign of security breach or attack Advanced

subcontrols: System utilisation and performance trends
Deviation from entity policy and procedures c. Access control variances and violations
Any potential sign of security breach or attack Advanced

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall synchronize clock of all information systems with an agreed time source 1. Standardize date/time format and enforce the standard time to be used in all systems

criteria.text: Ensure clock of medical devices and equipment are same as that of the connected systems 3. Regularly check that the clocks of all relevant information processing systems are synchronized. Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define and establish formal procedure for updates and patching of information system and application, medical devices and equipment 1. Ensure all systems and devices that process or communicate information are patched and protected

criteria.text: Define criteria and process for application of standard, urgent and critical patches 3. Ensure all critical security patches are applied as soon as practicable from the date of release.
Ensure patches are deployed to a subset of systems or devices to allow testing before deployment to all.
Ensure firmware on devices are updated
Periodically validate patch status of systems and devices in use Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall monitor information processing systems to prevent opportunities for information leakage 1. Implement data leakage prevention (DLP) measures Transitional

The healthcare entity shall conduct periodic independent assessment to ensure information assets are secure and always protected 1. Establish yearly schedules and conduct: a. Penetration testing on the entity’s system, network, applications and security infrastructures and environment b. Vulnerability assessment on all entity’s system, network, applications and  security infrastructures and environment c. Web security assessments on web applications accessible over internet

criteria.text: Establish processes to conduct security testing and authorization by authorized business and security stakeholders for new system/application and upgrades prior to production roll-out and usage 3. Establish processes to mitigate and manage identified findings and vulnerabilities
Share reports on identified findings and vulnerabilities and the status of mitigation with:
sub: The entity’s management
Department of Health, Abu Dhabi’s health sector regulator

criteria.text: Periodically follow up on the progress and status of mitigation measures with the appropriate stakeholders
Verify effectiveness and efficiency of mitigation measures Transitional

subcontrols: The entity’s management
Department of Health, Abu Dhabi’s health sector regulator

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure that assessment data is not available with third parties engaged to conduct assessments beyond the time of engagement 1. Ensure that system, network, applications and security related information is shared with third parties when they are on-site

criteria.text: Ensure that all information related to the entity’s system, network, applications and security infrastructures and environment and assessment outcomes are erased from the involved third party’s assets and environment after the completion of the assessment activity 3. Ensure that any shared reports are suitably protected through an adequate encryption mechanism Transitional

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, enforce and maintain a health information protection policy that ensures management’s commitment to protect healthcare information 1. Define management requirements on; a. Criteria for access and acceptable usage b. Accountability and/or data ownership c. Healthcare data communication or sharing

criteria.text: Mandate the requirements of non-disclosure and confidentiality during and after employment 3. Define government sanctions and legal obligations
Include reference to organizational disciplinary process Basic

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure that healthcare information under its custody is suitably protected 1. Conduct orientation on healthcare information protection and sanctions to all its employees, relevant contractors and third parties prior to their access to healthcare information

criteria.text: Establish stricter process to ensure clear desk and clear screen practices are adhered to in areas where healthcare information is used, processed or handled 3. Define and enforce criteria for healthcare information access
Ensure access to health information systems and applications are
